title: Football fans in Kerala transform their homes into colourful tributes to their teams
content: For these two fans, who live near Kochi, who have painted their houses in the colours of the countries they support in the FiFA World Cup, football is more than a seasonal obsession

title: National Medical Commission approves 100 additional MBBS seats for Tamil Nadu
content: It has permitted the government medical colleges in Namakkal and Tiruppur to increase their annual MBBS intake by 50 seats each from the academic year 2026-27

title: CJP protest day 23: Wangchuk’s health worsens as fast enters 15th day
content: The CJP stated that several political leaders and public intellectuals were scheduled to visit the protest site during the day to express solidarity with the agitation

title: Landslides will continue unless there is accountability, say scientists, environmentalists
content: They point out that the recent episodes are a result of a combination of geological factors, meteorological and topographical changes

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: Cloudburst floods hotels, houses in Pahalgam; heavy to very heavy rain lash parts of India
content: Continuous heavy rainfall on July 11 triggered a major landslide scare in Shimla’s Sanjauli area, forcing residents of Bothwell Estate to remain outdoors since around 3 a.m amid fears that their houses could collapse

title: Karnataka Transport Minister goes incognito on BMTC buses, orders action against staff
content: During the inspection, Mr. Suresh found a conductor and a driver allegedly derelict in their duties and recommended their suspension

title: VCK turns its focus to Tamil nationalism post-election
content: VCK founder and Chidambaram MP Thol. Thirumavalavan has urged the party to gear up for a ‘Tamil nationalist’ public rally on August 17 in Ulundurpet near Villupuram

title: ‘Punjab’s painful past is not a script to be selectively edited to suit a narrative,’ says Minister Bittu
content: “Makers of Satluj cannot hide behind the excuse of “creative freedom” while presenting disputed claims as established history,” says Ravneet Singh Bittu

title: Karnataka police clarify SC/ST grievance meetings are for the public, not police personnel
content: The police said the earlier reference to “police officers and personnel” was incorrect and has now been rectified. The meetings are exclusively intended as a public outreach initiative to safeguard the rights and welfare of SC/ST citizens.

title: How a single ₹500 counterfeit currency helped police unravel a larger syndicate
content: The kingpin and the accused arrested in Bengaluru allegedly printed the counterfeit currency and roped in foot soldiers to circulate the notes

title: MEA says 10 Indians rescued, one missing after vessel attacked off Oman
content: The MEA went on to describe as “deeply worrisome”, the “continuing incidents of attacks on commercial shipping in the region”

title: Vietnam boat tragedy: Injured A.P. tourist recovering, repatriation of mortal remains of the deceased underway
content: Embassy of India in Vietnam has finalised the authorised agency that will facilitate the repatriation of the mortal remains of the deceased to India

title: Supreme Court to hear plea seeking guidelines to curb 'unpredictable fluctuations' of airfares on July 13
content: A bench of Justices Vikram Nath and Sandeep Mehta will hear the plea filed by social activist S. Laxminarayanan, who also sought a robust and independent regulator that ensures transparency and passenger protection across the civil aviation sector

title: Tiruchi residents among those killed in Vietnam boat accident
content: N. Balaji, A.M. Sheikh Abdullah, and S. Alagurajan, were residents of Tiruchi who were killed in the Vietnam boat accident

title: Youth Congress worker assault case: Kerala Government seeks legal opinion to prosecute ADGP M.R. Ajith Kumar
content: ‘Home Minister Ramesh Chennithala has referred the legally and politically fraught question to the State Advocate General (AG)’s office based on a statement of facts filed by the charging officer in the case, Baiju Paulose, in the Alappuzha district court in June,’ says Officials

title: Police, bulldozers deployed against Amaravati land-givers, alleges Jagan Mohan Reddy
content: The YSRCP chief demands that the government should refrain from acquiring even a cent of land without the farmers’ consent

title: Tamil Nadu government seeks assistance from Union government for MRTS revamp in Chennai
content: ₹6,600-crore project is aimed at transforming  MRTS to Chennai Metro standards by refurbishing stations and upgrading trains; T.N. urges Union govt. to consider plan as ‘Central sector’ project

title: S. Janaki who had the voice for every human emotion
content: She possessed the rare ability to convey every shade of human emotion. Her songs remain woven into the cultural memory of south India.

title: S. Janaki: remembering the iconic voice of south India
content: Janaki sang in Hindi and Sinhala too, but it was in the South Indian languages that she carved a niche. Her rendition was infused with love and pathos, and was often a hat-tip to bygone times

title: Ensure pilgrim safety on Tirumala footpath after leopard sighting: Pawan Kalyan
content: The Deputy Chief Minister asked the Forest Department and TTD wings to coordinate, deploy H.A.N.U.M.A.N teams and clear thickets, and advised pilgrims on the footpath to move in groups

title: Naidu, Pawan Kalyan, Durgesh mourn S. Janaki’s demise
content: Chief Minister says the ‘Nightingale of South India’ built an unparalleled musical legacy spanning over six decades; Deputy Chief Minister and Cinematography Minister describe her death as an irreparable loss to Indian music.

title: Legendary singer S. Janaki will be alive through her songs, say Vizianagaram cultural groups
content: The singer received Jeevana Safalya Puraskaram (Lifetime Achievement Award) from Sri Guru Narayana Kalapeetham on April 1, 2026 in a function organised in Vizianagaram

title: S. Janaki (1938-2026) — A collection recalling the singer's pan-India career
content: S. Janaki won the National Film Award for Best Female Playback Singer for “Senthoora Poove” from 16 Vayathinile, the debut directorial venture of Bharathiraja, who passed away last month

title: S. Janaki’s fresh voice, improvisation made Kerala groove to her tunes
content: Janaki inimitably lent her voice to numerous evergreen Malayalam film songs from the 1960s to the 1990s. Songs such as Manjanikombil, Thenum Vayambum, Nadha Nee varum, Manassiluranroo, Akale Akale Neelakasham, Ilam Manjin have retained their freshness and ability to influence the listener.

title: NCP (SP) leader Eknath Khadse admits Delhi visit, but says will remain with Sharad Pawar
content: "I want to make it clear that I have no interest in joining the BJP now. I am with Sharad Pawar and will remain with him till the end. My workers may be interested in joining the BJP, but I am not," he asserted

title: Kerala remembers the divine voice of S. Janaki
content: Composers, lyricists and singers remember the legendary singer’s enchanting voice, pitch-perfect tonality and enduring influence

title: Hyderabad student missing in Finland for over two months found dead
content: "We have many doubts. After two months, the body has been recovered. We strongly feel that it is a suspicious death and not a natural death," says Manideep Reddy’s family lawyer

title: V.D. Satheesan condoles S. Janaki’s demise, says she was ‘the voice that captivated generations’
content: Kerala Chief Minister says her contributions to Indian film music transcended the title ‘the nightingale of south India’

title: S. Janaki: Not Kerala-born, but forever its voice
content: With her passing, Kerala mourns not merely a legendary playback singer from Andhra Pradesh, but an artiste whose songs dissolved linguistic boundaries and became woven into the emotional fabric of the State’s cultural life.

title: Pune building collapse: Toll up at 9 as last body recovered; official dubs incident 'act of God'
content: The body of Waman Kasbe, the last missing person, was recovered from the rubble in the wee hours of Sunday, raising the death toll to nine, while 14 persons have been rescued, officials said.

title: Pune building's compound wall collapses after rains; 14 vehicles damaged
content: "The residential society is located on Jambhulwadi Road in Katraj. The compound wall of the society collapsed onto the parking shed following incessant heavy rainfall. Several vehicles, including two-wheelers and four-wheelers, were damaged," a fire official said

title: When S. Janaki rejected the Padma Bhushan
content: “I should have been given the highest civilian award of Bharat Ratna if the government wanted to recognise my work. I am not interested in any other awards like the Padma awards,” Janaki said, after she was chosen for the Padma Bhushan

title: Delivery executive arrested for allegedly forcing entry into woman’s home, flashing at her
content: The accused has been identified as Vijay Mallikarjun Kamath (22); he has been arrested in connection with the incident that came to light after the survivor shared her ordeal and video footage of the confrontation on social media

title: Cloudburst floods hotels, houses in Pahalgam; no casualties
content: A sudden spell of intense rainfall, possibly caused by a localised cloudburst, struck the forest areas of Awoora and Dehwathu in Pahalgam, in south Kashmir's Anantnag district, says officials

title: Teeparru to be developed as model Pushkar village for Godavari Pushkaralu: Kandula Durgesh
content: Launching the Maa Godavari Sankalp programme, the Minister said the village works would be completed by Sankranti, and that ₹96 crore had been proposed to strengthen the 36-km embankment and improve 29 ghats

title: Bihar government launches doorstep land and property registration for citizens above 75 years old
content: Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary inaugurated this new system introduced by the Department of Prohibition, Excise, and Registration. This move spares the elderly the hassle of visiting registration offices, standing in long queues, or undertaking difficult journeys.

title: Punjab BJP accuses AAP of subverting the entire electoral process in urban local body polls
content: The ruling party first manipulated ward delimitation to suit its interests, then intimidated opposition candidates, snatched nomination papers and attempted to prevent them from contesting the elections, says BJP State President Kewal Singh Dhillon

title: Why did the U.S. indict Lawrence Bishnoi over Hardeep Singh Nijjar’s murder?
content: The U.S. has indicted Lawrence Bishnoi, a jailed Indian gangster, for his alleged role in a transnational conspiracy linked to the murder of Khalistani separatist Hardeep Singh Nijjar in Canada in June 2023.

title: Mayawati alleges Opposition using Dalit organisations to mislead Bahujan society
content: Congress party hit back adding the BSP chief is reading out from the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) playbook and weakening the Bahujan and marginalized cause

title: Moderate quake of magnitude 4.5 recorded in Bay of Bengal, off Andhra Pradesh
content: The quake originated at 05:05:46 IST at a depth of 10 km, with its epicentre located at 16.805° N, 84.381° E, the NCS said. The event has since been classified as “Reviewed” by the agency

title: Three Andhra Pradesh tourists among 15 Indians killed in Vietnam boat tragedy
content: Two persons from Kadapa and one from Machilipatnam are among the victims; Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu instructs officials to provide all possible assistance to the bereaved families and ensure the safe return of the survivors

title: Role of AI is only that of an assistant, not a judge, says Chief Justice of India
content: Whether digital dispute resolution and AI enhance or disrupt access to justice depends on how thoughtfully and deliberately their use is designed, says Chief Justice Kant

title: New Zealand PM Luxon hails India’s ‘incredible’ growth story, urges stronger business ties
content: Stressing that there are many areas for India and New Zealand to collaborate, Mr. Luxon named food and produce, tourism experiences, educational institutions, and technology as sectors for possible collaboration

title: Congress steps up Ram Temple campaign, asks Modi to answer for alleged donation theft
content: Congress party holds 26 press conferences across the country, holds Prime Minister Narendra Modi “directly answerable to the nation” as he had taken political ownership of the temple project

title: Veteran playback singer S. Janaki passes away
content: She was admitted to Apollo Hospital in Mysuru in the early hours of Saturday after she developed health complications.

title: Vietnam boat accident: Andhra Pradesh CM directs expedited return of affected tourists, mortal remains
content: Officials also told the Chief Minister that the Indian Embassy in Vietnam has engaged an agency to facilitate the handover and transportation of the bodies of the deceased

title: Pallapatla mourns its illustrious daughter S. Janaki
content: Residents recall the legendary singer’s childhood in the village as tributes pour in, with Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu saying her timeless voice touched generations

title: When Bollywood jived to S. Janaki’s infectious voice
content: In the 1980s, when there was a massive influx of South Indian production houses into Bollywood, remaking South Indian hits into Hindi, Janaki emerged as a pan-Indian voice

title: S. Janaki, the voice for every emotion, passes away
content: S. Janaki won the National Film Award for Best Female Playback Singer for “Senthoora Poove” from Bharathiraja’s 16 Vayathinile; had a celebrated collaboration with maestro Ilaiyaraaja

title: The nightingale who stole Kerala’s heart
content: Janaki’s effortless singing and pleasant nature earned her enduring love and admiration across generations

title: A lullaby falls silent: Kannada cinema bids farewell to S. Janaki
content: Among the tributes that poured in from the Kannada music and film fraternities, one sentiment stood out: her extraordinary versatility

title: News in Frames | A sacred trail of faith and fellowship
content: The centuries-old Amarnath Yatra, a Hindu pilgrimage through the Himalayas’ treacherous terrain, is more than a religious tradition as it brings thousands of people on a shared path; the 57-day journey, which also supports the livelihoods of many, carries a powerful message of harmony and compassion in an increasingly polarised world

title: Gym belt matches Twisha Sharma's injuries, says AIIMS report
content: An AIIMS Delhi team has submitted an 11-page report to the CBI. Laboratory examinations confirmed skin tissue on the gymnastics belt. This finding matched the injury pattern on Twisha Sharma's neck. The report was submitted in a sealed cover under court orders. This development is significant for the ongoing investigation into the death.

title: Daughter-in-law accuses former MP Vinayak Raut, family of harassment
content: Vinayak Raut and six others face charges following a complaint by his daughter-in-law. Girija Raut alleged physical and mental harassment after her marriage in December 2017. She claimed occult rituals and unidentified pills were administered to help her conceive. Girija stated prolonged mental harassment prevented her from speaking out earlier. Police have arrested one accused, Firoz Baba, and are investigating further.

title: China leaps ahead with reusable rocket booster, India’s NGLV years away
content: China demonstrated orbital rocket booster recovery, joining reusable launch system developers. India's Next Generation Launch Vehicle program is still years from a demonstration flight. This achievement narrows the gap with global leaders like SpaceX and Blue Origin. Recovering and reusing rocket stages significantly reduces launch costs and increases frequency. India is committed to reusable launchers through its NGLV program.

title: Indian record holder Tejas Nandakumar barred from CWG 2026: 'I'm devastated'
content: India's national record holder Tejas Nandakumar has been ruled out of the 2026 Commonwealth Games despite qualifying, after his para sport classification failed to meet eligibility requirements. Speaking exclusively to TimesofIndia.com, the 20-year-old reveals how administrative delays, despite months of international travel and personal expense, shattered his Glasgow dream, while urging systemic reforms to prevent similar heartbreak for future para athletes.

title: Jude makes England's World cup song better as Messi looms...
content: Day 31 saw Jude Bellingham drag England past Norway, Argentina stumble through Switzerland and Lionel Messi demand respect from the referee.

title: Apple lawsuit reveals what ex-iPhone engineer allegedly took before joining OpenAI
content: Apple has filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of stealing confidential trade secrets.  The company alleges a former engineer took sensitive files and knowledge after leaving.  OpenAI allegedly encouraged job candidates to share Apple's proprietary information.  This information was reportedly used to accelerate OpenAI's own hardware projects.  OpenAI denies any interest in acquiring other companies' trade secrets.

title: 'If that's a free kick...': Haaland fumes over VAR call in World Cup exit
content: Erling Haaland's World Cup debut ended with Norway's quarterfinal loss to England. He scored seven goals, establishing himself as a top tournament scorer. Haaland described the experience as unforgettable and the coolest six weeks of his life. He believes Norway's performance has put the nation on the football map. The striker is now looking forward to a well-deserved break.

title: Ayodhya to Tirupati: When temple donations go missing
content: Major Indian temples face scrutiny over financial management practices.  Sabarimala and Tirumala temples reported significant gold and cash theft incidents.  Badrinath and Kedarnath temples also experienced allegations of donation pilfering.  Jagannath Temple has seen multiple cash and idol theft cases over years.  These events highlight systemic issues in safeguarding temple revenues.

title: Hours after Zuckerberg posted about AI model on Musk’s X, he gets business proposal
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced the new Muse Spark 1.1 AI model on X. X's Head of Product Nikita Bier then offered Creator Monetization for his account. This exchange occurred on Elon Musk's platform after Zuckerberg's AI announcement. The Muse Spark 1.1 model is designed for coding and complex AI tasks. Developers can now access this powerful new AI through Meta's API.

title: Burning lemon peels and cloves: The surprising benefits explained
content: For centuries, the practice of burning lemon peels and cloves has provided households with protection from sickness while effectively concealing unpleasant odors. Cloves, rich in eugenol, boast antimicrobial and insect-repellent properties, while the delightful citrus scent from lemon peel oil contains d-limonene, known for its calming effects. However, those with respiratory issues should approach this tradition with caution due to the smoke's potential impact.

title: How floating solar panels gave thousands of Salmon a new home
content: A Chilean salmon farm replaced diesel generators with floating solar panels. This initiative significantly cut emissions and fuel consumption annually. The new system also created a quieter and more sheltered environment for the fish. Thousands of salmon have now gathered beneath the floating solar structures. This project demonstrates renewable energy's unexpected environmental benefits beyond emission reduction.

title: Jeff Bezos' ex-wife MacKenzie Scott donates to US youth mental health non-profit
content: MacKenzie Scott has donated twenty million dollars to Active Minds. This significant contribution is the largest in the nonprofit's history. Active Minds focuses on youth mental health awareness and reducing stigma. The unrestricted funds will expand programs and support young leaders. This donation occurs amid growing concerns about youth mental health.

title: Beyond Patriot: Why Ukraine is pushing Europe to build its own missile shield
content: Kyiv is currently under intensified drone and missile assaults from Russia, placing significant pressure on Ukraine's air defence systems. President Zelenskyy is calling on allies to expedite the delivery of weapons, highlighting a critical need for Patriot systems. Europe’s dependence on US Patriot interceptors for missile defence has revealed vulnerabilities in production and stockpiles. Strengthening domestic capabilities is vital for securing Europe's defence in the future.

title: Lionel Messi's FIFA World Cup scoring streak ends; fails to ...
content: Lionel Messi provided a crucial assist for Argentina's opening goal against Switzerland. The defending champions secured a 3-1 extra-time victory in their quarterfinal match. Messi's nine-game scoring streak ended, but his influence remained evident. Argentina now advances to face England in the semifinals of the tournament. Coach Scaloni praised Messi's consistent performance and dedication to excellence.

title: Bengaluru delivery agent arrested for sexually harassing woman inside her flat
content: A delivery executive has been arrested in Bengaluru for alleged sexual harassment. The woman stated he entered her flat without permission after asking to use the washroom. She reported he ignored her objections and exposed himself after using the facility. The incident left her feeling violated and unsafe in her own home. Police have initiated legal action and are continuing their investigation into the matter.

title: Will TFPC fine 'Jana Nayagan' makers? Full-page ad sparks debate
content: Jana Nayagan's makers face scrutiny over a full-page newspaper advertisement. The Tamil Film Producers Council's rules are central to this ongoing industry debate. Questions arise about whether regulations will be applied equally to all producers. The film's release is anticipated soon, with high expectations from audiences. All eyes are now on the TFPC's decision regarding the promotional strategy.

title: Shreyas Iyer joins unwanted list after seventh straight defeat as India captain
content: Shreyas Iyer's captaincy saw another loss as England secured a dominant series victory.  England posted a massive 257 runs, powered by Buttler and Brook's record partnership.  India's bowlers struggled significantly throughout the entire innings.  Chasing a large target, India's batting showed some fight but fell short.  This defeat ended India's long reign at the top of T20I rankings.

title: 7-year-old girl raped and killed at Ghaziabad mall site, minor among 2 detained
content: A seven-year-old girl was found murdered in a Ghaziabad mall construction site.  Her brothers saw two men offer her chips and a cold drink.  Police detained two suspects, one a juvenile, identified through CCTV footage.  The accused allegedly sexually assaulted the child and threw her body down a shaft.  Her body was recovered nearly five hours after she went missing.

title: Watch: Passenger, TC clash over Marathi language on Mumbai local
content: Railway authorities are investigating a viral video of an argument on a Mumbai local train. The dispute reportedly began after a passenger spoke in Marathi with railway staff. A ticket checker intervened, and both parties later approached the police. The railway is examining the video and the overall incident. Suitable action will be taken after the preliminary investigation concludes.

title: 'I can sit here and cry, but...': Norway coach breaks silence on Bellingham goal controversy
content: Norway's coach Stale Solbakken believes the ball hit an overhead camera cable before Jude Bellingham's goal. He stated the referee did not see the incident and received no official notification. FIFA later claimed no evidence showed the ball touching the wire and altering its trajectory. Despite the controversy, Solbakken praised his players' tournament performance and accepted the outcome.

title: Beyond the Quad: How India is expanding its defence arc across the Indo-Pacific
content: Prime Minister Modi's Indo-Pacific visits highlight India's growing defense ties. India strengthens its Indo-Pacific strategy through diverse bilateral partnerships. Cooperation with nations like Vietnam and Indonesia focuses on maritime security. India's defense exports and naval presence are increasing across the region. These efforts position India as a stabilizing force in the Indo-Pacific.

title: SI suspended for not arresting Pocso accused who killed 6 in overnight rampage
content: Family members alleged police negligence in a Pocso case, leading to a brutal murder. Sub-inspector T Ramesh was suspended, and inspector B Kantha Reddy received a charge memo. The family also suspects the accused's involvement in the father's death a year prior. Police stated the father's death was due to ill health and the accused was counselled. The government sanctioned Rs 5 lakh ex gratia for the victim's sister.

title: India hit the ground running with Kohli, Gill holding a lengthy conversation
content: Virat Kohli, Shubman Gill, Rohit Sharma and KL Rahul were the batters involved in the session, while Jasprit Bumrah, Kuldeep Yadav and Gurnoor Brar led the bowling group. India also used the services of local net bowlers, including two spinners and a fast bowler, to replicate different match scenarios.

title: Indian-origin tech leader killed by husband in Georgia, wounded son runs to neighbors
content: In a shocking domestic violence incident in Georgia, a man is charged after allegedly shooting his son and killing his wife. The injured son sought refuge with neighbors while Sheetal Wrzesien, the victim, a tech leader, was lying dead inside the house. The husband was arrested and charged with felony murder.

title: Cracks appear on Delhi-Doon eway ramp
content: Fresh reports of soil subsidence and cracks have emerged on the Delhi-Dehradun Expressway. A ramp near Shamli's Baburi area shows damage after continuous rainfall. The National Highways Authority of India has formed a special investigation team to inspect these areas. This audit aims to ensure transparency and address safety concerns before the monsoon intensifies. Motorists and local villagers express worry over the expressway's urgent need for repairs.

title: FIFA World Cup: Alvarez's extra-time strike sends Argentina into semis
content: Defending champions Argentina secured a 3-1 victory over Switzerland after extra time. Alexis Mac Allister opened the scoring for Argentina in the tenth minute. Switzerland equalized in the second half, but a red card changed the match dynamics. Julian Alvarez and Lautaro Martinez scored in extra time to seal the win. Argentina will now face England in the semifinals on Wednesday.

title: 'Asked mom for Rs 6,000': Mystery shrouds Hyderabad student's death in Finland
content: Finnish authorities officially declared Manideep Reddy Gujja dead after his disappearance in May. An unidentified body recovered from the sea was identified through fingerprint analysis. The recovered wallet contained Manideep's identity card and his mother's credit card. Finnish police found no evidence of criminal activity in the case. The family has requested an independent investigation into the circumstances.

title: 'I graduated with C's and D's': Harvard PhD's reminder that grades don't define future
content: Dr. Joel Tan shared his journey from low high school grades to a Harvard PhD. He explained that a university's belief in his potential changed his entire life. Tan highlighted that talent exists everywhere, but opportunities are not equally distributed. He urged fellow graduates to open doors for others as they were once helped. His message stressed that a single chance can enable individuals to achieve their goals.

title: Bengaluru woman alleges delivery agent forced way into home to use washroom, flashed her
content: A Bengaluru woman reported a delivery agent entered her flat without permission. He allegedly exposed himself after she refused his toilet request multiple times. The woman felt shocked and unsafe within her own home. She stated that a simple refusal should have ended the interaction. No police complaint has been filed regarding this disturbing incident.

title: Mosque prayers suspended at Kolkata airport; here's why
content: Access to Bankra mosque is suspended for two days due to urgent road repairs. Devotees may resume prayers on Monday if work concludes as scheduled. Local residents expressed surprise at the extended restriction for repairs. Officials believe the mosque poses a serious threat to aviation security. Relocation talks for the mosque have reportedly resumed after past rejections.

title: Harry Kane, Jude Bellingham script FIFA World Cup history, become first pair to...
content: Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham achieved a historic World Cup scoring milestone for England. Bellingham scored twice in the quarterfinal victory, securing a semifinal berth for the team. Their combined six goals each set a new tournament benchmark for teammates from one nation. England defeated Norway in extra time, ending Haaland's scoring streak. The Three Lions now face Argentina in their quest for a World Cup final.

title: How Chinese-made PX-30 pistols reached shooters from Pakistan's black market under scanner
content: Guns recovered from a Gurgaon encounter are believed to be Chinese Norinco semi-automatics. These sophisticated firearms are often smuggled into India through illegal channels. Police are investigating the supply chain and potential links to organized gangs. Gangster Deepak Nandal is suspected of sending shooters armed with these pistols. The investigation aims to uncover the full extent of the international arms smuggling network.

title: No change: Conductor tells Karnataka transport minister to get off bus
content: Transport minister Byrathi Suresh experienced commuter problems firsthand. A bus conductor asked him to exit for lacking exact change. He also encountered an auto-rickshaw driver demanding an inflated fare. These incidents occurred during a surprise inspection of city bus services. The minister traveled incognito to assess public transport conditions.

title: Ship catches fire off Oman hours after US strike; British crew escapes on lifeboat
content: The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) said the damaged vessel was located east of Oman and confirmed that the crew had safely evacuated. "UKMTO has been informed by military authorities and the CSO of the vessel that the crew have abandoned the vessel and are currently embarked in a lifeboat," the British maritime agency said.

title: 9 dead in Pune's Moshi building collapse; waste plant operator chief blames heavy rainfall
content: Seven more bodies were recovered from the collapsed building at the Moshi waste-to-energy plant. This recovery brought the total death toll from the incident to eight people. One person remained missing after the structure collapsed on Wednesday afternoon. Rescue teams worked tirelessly for over 79 hours to locate the missing individual. Compensation has been announced for the families of the victims.

title: Lionel Messi scripts FIFA World Cup history, becomes first player to...
content: Lionel Messi achieved a historic FIFA World Cup milestone on Saturday. He became the first player with ten assists in the tournament's history. This remarkable feat occurred during Argentina's quarterfinal match against Switzerland. Messi's assist moved him past Diego Maradona on the all-time charts. Argentina continues its pursuit of a fourth World Cup title.

title: Former Qatar Emir Sheikh Hamad Bin Khalifa Al Thani Dies At 74
content: Sheikh Hamad, who stepped down in June 2013 after 18 years as emir, was the architect of energy-rich Qatar's stunning ambitions that turned it from a backwater into an international crossroads in less than a generation.

title: Earthquake Of Magnitude 4.5 Hits Bay Of Bengal, Tremors Felt In Andhra
content: The tremors were felt in Gajuwaka, Mangalapalem, Sagar Nagar, Pedda Waltair, Arilova, MVP colony, Appughar and other areas.

title: 'Dawood Planted Bomb At Taj': Hoax Call Triggers Security Scare In Mumbai
content: According to officials, the Taj Hotel was placed on high alert after, at around 12:13 am, a man called the Navi Mumbai Police Control Room and allegedly claimed, "Dawood has planted a bomb at the Taj Hotel".

title: Indian Missing, 10 Rescued After Iran Attacks Ship In Strait Of Hormuz
content: India described the continued attacks on ships as deeply worrisome and called for an immediate de-escalation in the Middle East.

title: Why The Amarnath Ice Lingam Is Melting Faster Than Ever Before
content: According to several reports, the ice lingam has melted by over 90% and has almost disappeared just days into the 57-day Amarnath yatra.

title: Delivery Agent Who Barged Into Bengaluru Woman's Home, Flashed Her, Arrested
content: The woman alleged that the delivery agent had visited her house to deliver a package and then asked her if he could use the toilet.

title: Iran Shuts Hormuz After Attack On 'Unauthorised' Ship, US Begins New Strikes
content: Iran said the Strait of Hormuz was closed "until further notice" and until "the end of US interference in this region."

title: Andhra Pradesh On Alert After 2 COVID-19 Deaths, 8 Active Cases
content: A 52-year-old man from Kadapa's Rajampet area, who tested positive for COVID-19 after developing fever and cough, died while undergoing treatment at Christian Medical College (CMC), Vellore.

title: Skin Tissue On Gym Belt Matches Twisha Sharma's Neck Injuries: AIIMS Report
content: Actress-model Twisha Sharma was found dead at her matrimonial home on May 12, within six months of her marriage to advocate Samarth Singh.

title: How Terrorist Groups Are Using AI To Plan Attacks And Make Bombs
content: Some terrorist groups, including Al Qaeda, Islamic State, and Boko Haram, are using AI tools to help plan attacks and make explosives.

title: "Scam Altman" vs "Elon Is Obsessed With Me": Musk And OpenAI CEO Clash Again
content: Elon Musk, who co-founded OpenAI in 2015 before leaving the company, has repeatedly accused OpenAI of abandoning its original nonprofit mission.

title: How OpenAI Engineer's 'LOL' Moment Triggered Legal Fight With Apple
content: Apple has sued OpenAI and two former employees, alleging misappropriation of its trade secrets to benefit the ChatGPT-owner's foray into consumer hardware.

title: 35 Years After Borrowing Rs 1,000, Kerala Man Returns Rs 25,000 To Ex-Colleague With Interest
content: Ismail and Lachanna became friends in 1991 while working in Abqaiq, Saudi Arabia. They shared accommodation with three other expatriate workers and lived together for nearly five years.

title: US Hits 140 Iranian Targets After Hormuz Closure, Tehran Strikes UAE, Qatar
content: Iran announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz after firing on a vessel it said was taking an "unauthorised route".

title: Hyderabad Student Missing In Finland For Over 2 Months Found Dead In Sea
content: The family said that Manideep Reddy, 18, spoke with his mother shortly before he went missing in Helsinki.

title: Vietnam Boat Tragedy Updates: Vietnam Orders Probe After Boat Tragedy Kills 15 Indian Tourists

title: Trump's Modification Of Endangered Species Act Will Allow Harm To Habitats
